description: Curated reference anchors for the scoring-validation experiment. Every affinity is a real, citable number (median Ki/Kd) from the ChEMBL activity API (standard_type in {Ki, Kd}, standard_units = nM); pKd = -log10(Kd*1e-9). None are invented. Selection principle: reversible, drug-like, near-neutral, single defined pocket in one well-behaved docking regime (ATP-competitive kinase hinge binders + one clean soluble-enzyme inhibitor), matching the project's oncology domain. Deliberately consistent, not maximally broad.
affinity_source: ChEMBL activity API (https://www.ebi.ac.uk/chembl/), median of Ki/Kd measurements in nM.
target_query_note: target = gene symbol used for structure resolution (KDR = VEGFR2, F10 = FXa).
scope_limitation: Every anchor is a potent approved drug (pKd 7.36-10.09); there are no clean, well-dockable weak binders in this set (the obvious weak candidates - fragments, GPCR ligands, charged/covalent chemistry - are cases where docking scores are unreliable). The affinity range is therefore compressed relative to the ligand-size range, which is precisely why size confounds the scores. Extending to a reliable weak, well-dockable anchor is documented follow-up work.
